zh.lifeSummary: Juana García Carrillo是García Gómez Carrillo de Molina和Elvira Álvarez Osorio的女兒，於1269年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安瓦拉阿多利德維利亞巴聶斯。她於1299年與Diego Gutiérrez de Ceballos在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1386年在西班牙享年117歲，埋葬在西班牙坎塔布里亞埃斯卡蘭特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Juana García Carrillo是García Gómez Carrillo de Molina和Elvira Álvarez Osorio的女儿，于1269年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安瓦拉阿多利德維利亞巴聶斯。她于1299年与Diego Gutiérrez de Ceballos在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1386年在西班牙享年117岁，埋葬在西班牙坎塔布里亞埃斯卡蘭特。

nameMeanings.name: Carrillo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Carlos, Jesus, Manuel, Luis, Pedro, Francisco, Ruben, Miguel, Roberto, Mario.

Spanish: nickname for a person with some peculiarity of the cheek or jaw, Spanish carrillo. The word appears to be a diminutive of carro ‘cart, wagon’, and it has been suggested that the reference is to the movements of the jaw in chewing. The surname may also have denoted originally a bold or shameless person; for the semantic development compare Cheek .
Italian (Campania): probably from a diminutive of carro (see Carro ), unless it derives from the Spanish (see 1 above).

variants.name: Cheek
meanings: English: from Middle English cheeke (Old English cē(a)ce), used as a nickname for someone with some deformity or scar in the region of the cheek or jawbone. Compare Cheak and Chick .

nameMeanings.name: Juana
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: feminine form of   Juan .  It is now also occasionally used in the English-speaking world, to which it was introduced mainly by Hispanic settlers in the United States.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: García
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Often used as a feminine equivalent of Mark , but in fact a feminine form of Marcius, itself a derivative of Marcus . One St Marcia is commemorated in a group with Felix, Luciolus, Fortunatus, and others; another with Zenais, Cyria, and Valeria; and a third with Ariston, Crescentian, Eutychian, Urban, Vitalis, Justus, Felicissimus, Felix, and Symphorosa. None is individually very famous.

variants.name: Marsha
meanings: Phonetic spelling of   Marcia  , associated particularly with the American film star  Marsha Hunt  (  b. 1917 ).

en.lifeSummary: Juana García Carrillo was born in 1269, in Villabáñez, Valladolid, Castilla y León, Spain as the daughter of García Gómez Carrillo de Molina and Elvira Álvarez Osorio. She married Diego Gutiérrez de Ceballos in 1299, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1386, in Spain, at the age of 117, and was buried in Escalante, Cantabria, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Juana García Carrillo est née en 1269, à Villabáñez, Valladolid, Castilla y León, Espagne, fille de García Gómez Carrillo de Molina et Elvira Álvarez Osorio. Elle a épousé Diego Gutiérrez de Ceballos en 1299, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1386, à Espagne, à l’âge de 117 ans, et a été enterrée à Escalante, Cantabria, Espagne.

ko.lifeSummary: Juana García Carrillo(은)는 1269년 스페인 카스틸라 와이 레o 발라도리드 비라브an즈에서 García Gómez Carrillo de Molina(와)과 Elvira Álvarez Osorio의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1299년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 Diego Gutiérrez de Ceballos(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1386년 스페인에서 117세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 캔타브리아 스알랜티에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Diego Gutiérrez de Ceballos em 1299, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.Villabáñez, Valladolid, Castilla y León,: виллабanез, валладолид, кастилла и леoн, Испания
ru.lifeSummary: Juana García Carrillo родилась в 1269 г. в местности виллабanез, валладолид, кастилла и леoн, Испания. Её родителей звали García Gómez Carrillo de Molina и Elvira Álvarez Osorio. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Diego Gutiérrez de Ceballos в 1299 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1386 г. в местности Испания в возрасте 117 лет и была похоронена в местности эскалант, кантабря, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Juana García Carrillo nació en 1269, en Villabáñez, Valladolid, Castilla y León, España como hija de García Gómez Carrillo de Molina y Elvira Álvarez Osorio. Se casó con Diego Gutiérrez de Ceballos en 1299, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1386, en España, a la edad de 117 años, y fue sepultada en Escalante, Cantabria, España.
